meaning: n.一阵一阵痛,如刺一样痛,剧痛vt.使一阵一阵痛,刺痛vt.感到剧痛,刺痛
examples: He felt a slight twinge in his damaged hamstring.
For a moment, Arnold felt a twinge of sympathy for Mr. Wilson.
I felt a twinge of guilt.
Most of us feel a twinge of guilt when we gossip about our co-workers.
When I look at Real Simple or a West Elm catalogue, I feel a twinge of inadequacy.
